title: Fix finalizer/PushSecret race leaking keys in OpenBao
description:
# GitHub Issue #271: Race between operator finalizer Delete and ESO PushSecret adoption leaks Fernet and credential keys in OpenBao

**Category**: bug | **Scope**: Medium

**Description**: The openbao-finalizer introduced in CC-0079 (PR #260, commit 09029b9) has a race with ESO's PushSecret adoption that can leak Fernet and credential key material in OpenBao KV-v2. `finalizeOpenBaoSecrets` (`operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_secrets.go:185-197`) calls `r.Delete` on `<keystone>-fernet-keys-backup` and `<keystone>-credential-keys-backup` without first confirming that ESO has adopted the PushSecret by installing its own finalizer (`pushsecret.externalsecrets.external-secrets.io/finalizer`). If the operator's Delete races ahead of ESO's first reconcile — for example because the ESO PushSecret workqueue is backed up under parallel load — Kubernetes removes the PushSecret object immediately (no finalizer to hold it) and ESO never reaches the `DeletionPolicy=Delete` branch that would issue `DeleteSecret` against the remote. The KV-v2 paths `kv-v2/openstack/keystone/fernet-keys` and `kv-v2/openstack/keystone/credential-keys` remain populated, carry `custom_metadata: managed-by=external-secrets` but no `deletion_time`, and — because there is no PushSecret left to reference them — stay orphaned until manual intervention.

**Motivation**: Reproduced on `main` in CI run [24842115250/job 72722485985](https://github.com/C5C3/forge/actions/runs/24842115250/job/72722485985) (commit 4526cd7). The `keystone-deletion-cleanup` E2E step-6 asserts both KV-v2 paths are `not-found` after PushSecret deletion; both still contained `version: 1` data with `created_time` `2026-04-23T15:27:51.982Z` (Fernet) and `2026-04-23T15:27:52.089Z` (credential) — i.e., ESO's first push landed 13 s AFTER `Ready=True` (`15:27:38`) and only ~1.6 s before the PushSecret was garbage-collected at `15:27:53.6`. The immediately preceding CI run [24832475387](https://github.com/C5C3/forge/actions/runs/24832475387) on the PR merge commit 09029b9 was green — only a `golang:1.25` Docker-digest bump (#261) lay between the two runs — confirming this is a timing-sensitive race, not a regression from the digest bump.

The blast radius is not limited to the E2E test: in production, a user who creates a Keystone CR and deletes it within seconds (CI automation, GitOps apply-then-revert, misconfigured HelmRelease, prematurely-cancelled CR) would leak Fernet tokens and credential-encryption keys in OpenBao — violating REQ-010 (CC-0079) and the Deletion Cleanup guarantee documented in `docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md`. A compromised KV read path would then expose historical token-signing material that the cluster administrator believes was purged.

Both Fernet and credential paths share the same defect and must be fixed symmetrically:

- **Fernet**: `fernetKeysPushSecret` (`reconcile_fernet.go:403-429`) targets `RemoteKey: openstack/keystone/fernet-keys`. Leak surface: active Fernet signing keys used to mint Keystone tokens; stale copies enable offline token forgery if the KV backend is later compromised.
- **Credential**: `credentialKeysPushSecret` (`reconcile_credential.go:409-435`) targets `RemoteKey: openstack/keystone/credential-keys`. Leak surface: credential-encryption keys used to protect OpenStack service credentials (EC2 access-keys, application credentials) at rest in the Keystone DB; stale copies enable offline decryption of any persisted credentials.

Both builders construct PushSecrets with identical shape — same `DeletionPolicy=Delete`, same `ClusterSecretStoreRef=openbao-cluster-store`, same `EnsurePushSecret` call site via `internal/common/secrets/secrets.go:128` — so one code path fix covers both resources. Neither `reconcileFernetKeys` nor `reconcileCredentialKeys` gates its `*Ready` condition on the PushSecret's own `status.conditions[type=Ready]`, so there is no upstream readiness gate that naturally prevents a user's Delete from overtaking ESO's first push.

**Affected Areas**:
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_secrets.go` (`finalizeOpenBaoSecrets`, `openBaoBackupPushSecretNames`, `setOpenBaoFinalizerBlockedCondition`)
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_fernet.go` (`fernetKeysPushSecret`, step 7 of `reconcileFernetKeys`)
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_credential.go` (`credentialKeysPushSecret`, the symmetric step in `reconcileCredentialKeys`)
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go` (`reconcileDeleteOpenBao` — new blocked reason surfaces here)
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go` (deletion-cleanup cases currently assume ESO finalizer is always present when the handler runs)
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_secrets_test.go` (new unit cases for the adoption wait)
- `tests/e2e/keystone/deletion-cleanup/chainsaw-test.yaml` (step-6 needs a bounded retry to tolerate residual ESO `DeleteSecret` latency after step-4 observes PushSecret gone)
- `docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md` (document the adoption wait + the new `WaitingForESOAdoption` reason)

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] `finalizeOpenBaoSecrets` performs a `Get` on each backup PushSecret before issuing `Delete`; for any PushSecret whose `DeletionTimestamp` is zero AND whose finalizer list does not contain `pushsecret.externalsecrets.external-secrets.io/finalizer`, the handler records `SecretsReady=False/OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked` with reason `WaitingForESOAdoption` (distinct from the existing `OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked` reason for post-Delete Terminating waits) and returns `(done=false, nil)` without issuing Delete.
- [ ] Once ESO adoption is observed (ESO finalizer present OR `DeletionTimestamp` already set on the PushSecret), the handler issues Delete exactly as today and Pass-2 semantics (`Get` NotFound → success) remain unchanged.
- [ ] Adoption check covers both `fernet-keys-backup` and `credential-keys-backup` via the existing `openBaoBackupPushSecretNames` iteration — no duplication per resource.
- [ ] New unit tests in `reconcile_secrets_test.go`:
  - PushSecret without ESO finalizer, no DeletionTimestamp → `finalizeOpenBaoSecrets` returns `(false, nil)`, blocked condition reason = `WaitingForESOAdoption`, no Delete call issued (verified via fake-client action log).
  - Same PushSecret with ESO finalizer added → Delete is issued once per call; Get-NotFound on second pass yields `(true, nil)`.
  - Mixed state (Fernet adopted, credential not) → blocked condition names the unadopted resource, no Delete issued on either; subsequent pass after adoption completes both.
- [ ] New integration test in `integration_test.go` covers the "Keystone CR deleted before ESO adopts PushSecret" path, verifying that (a) Keystone stays in Terminating with `SecretsReady=False/OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked/WaitingForESOAdoption`, (b) once the test harness adds the ESO finalizer to both backup PushSecrets the operator proceeds to Delete, (c) Keystone eventually finalizes, (d) the envtest-backed PushSecret objects reach `NotFound`.
- [ ] Chainsaw step-6 (`tests/e2e/keystone/deletion-cleanup/chainsaw-test.yaml:217-252`) replaces the one-shot `bao kv get` with a bounded retry loop (e.g., 30 s total, 3 s interval) so residual `DeleteSecret` latency between step-4 (PushSecret gone) and the OpenBao check does not re-flake — the operator fix plus a tolerant assertion together.
- [ ] `docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md` documents: (i) why the adoption wait exists (ESO PushSecret workqueue race), (ii) the new `WaitingForESOAdoption` reason alongside the existing blocked reason, (iii) the two-stage finalizer lifecycle (wait-for-adoption → Delete → wait-for-gone), (iv) that both Fernet and credential PushSecrets are covered by the same handler.
- [ ] No expansion of the RBAC surface — `delete` on `pushsecrets` remains the only extra verb already granted by #260. Adding `get` on `pushsecrets` is already covered by the existing rule.
- [ ] CI `chainsaw/keystone-deletion-cleanup` is green across 10 consecutive runs (manual `gh workflow run` trigger) to confirm flake resolution.

**Labels:** bug

**Labels:** bug

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/271

stories.title: Operator waits for ESO adoption before deleting backup PushSecrets (CC-0091)
stories.want: the Keystone openbao-finalizer to confirm that ESO has adopted each backup PushSecret (installed its own cleanup finalizer) before the operator issues Delete
stories.so_that: a Keystone CR deleted seconds after creation cannot race ahead of ESO's first reconcile and orphan Fernet or credential key material in OpenBao KV-v2
criteria: finalizeOpenBaoSecrets performs a Get on each of the two backup PushSecrets before any Delete is issued

If a PushSecret exists, has no DeletionTimestamp, and does not carry pushsecret.externalsecrets.external-secrets.io/finalizer, the handler records SecretsReady=False with reason WaitingForESOAdoption and returns (done=false, nil) without calling Delete
Once the ESO finalizer appears (or DeletionTimestamp is already set), the handler proceeds to the existing Delete + Get-NotFound passes unchanged
Adoption check covers both fernet-keys-backup and credential-keys-backup via the existing openBaoBackupPushSecretNames iteration — no per-resource duplication

stories.title: SRE sees a distinct condition reason when the finalizer is waiting for ESO adoption (CC-0091)
stories.want: the SecretsReady condition to distinguish 'waiting for ESO to adopt the PushSecret' from 'waiting for ESO to finish the remote DeleteSecret'
stories.so_that: I can tell from `kubectl describe keystone` alone whether the operator is stuck pre-Delete (ESO workqueue backlog) or post-Delete (OpenBao unreachable), without reading controller logs
criteria: The new reason constant is WaitingForESOAdoption, distinct from the existing OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked
The blocked-condition message names the specific PushSecret whose adoption is missing
ObservedGeneration is set to keystone.Generation on the condition, matching the existing pattern
Both reasons use the same Type=SecretsReady and Status=False so the top-level status surface does not regress

stories.title: Symmetric coverage for Fernet and credential backup PushSecrets (CC-0091)
stories.want: the adoption wait to be enforced symmetrically for both fernet-keys-backup and credential-keys-backup
stories.so_that: neither Fernet token-signing material nor credential-encryption keys can be leaked in OpenBao through the same race
criteria: Both PushSecret names from openBaoBackupPushSecretNames are checked in a single loop with no resource-specific branches

A mixed state (Fernet adopted, credential not) blocks on the unadopted resource — no Delete is issued on either in the same pass to preserve the existing 'fire all deletes in parallel' Pass-1 property once adoption is confirmed
A dedicated unit test asserts the mixed-state behavior: the condition message names the unadopted resource and no Delete action appears in the fake-client action log for either resource
Once both are adopted on a later pass, both are deleted in parallel per the existing Pass-1 semantics

stories.title: Chainsaw E2E deletion-cleanup test tolerates residual DeleteSecret latency (CC-0091)
stories.want: step-6 of tests/e2e/keystone/deletion-cleanup/chainsaw-test.yaml to retry the `bao kv get` check in a bounded loop
stories.so_that: a normal 1-3 s gap between step-4 (PushSecret gone) and the OpenBao remote DeleteSecret completing does not re-flake the test even with the operator fix in place
criteria: Step-6 wraps the existing `bao kv get` loop in a bounded retry: at most 30 s total wall-clock, 3 s interval, exit early on first success (both paths not-found)
Failure message on timeout still reports the specific kv-v2 path(s) that remained present
Script still returns a non-zero exit code when the path is present after the timeout, so the chainsaw step fails clearly
No change to step-4: PushSecret absence remains a hard assertion at that step

stories.title: Developer understands the adoption-wait addition without reading the diff (CC-0091)
stories.want: docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md to document the adoption wait as part of the openbao-finalizer lifecycle
stories.so_that: someone triaging a future 'Keystone stuck Terminating' incident can tell at a glance which of the two blocked reasons they are looking at and why
criteria: The openbao-finalizer section (existing lines 700-874) is extended, not replaced
The doc names both condition reasons: WaitingForESOAdoption (pre-Delete adoption wait) and OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked (post-Delete gone-wait)
The two-stage lifecycle is described: (1) wait for ESO adoption, (2) Delete, (3) wait for gone
The doc explicitly states that both Fernet and credential PushSecrets are covered by the same handler via openBaoBackupPushSecretNames

stories.title: No RBAC expansion (CC-0091)
stories.want: the fix to use only verbs already granted to the operator ServiceAccount
stories.so_that: the bug fix does not widen the blast radius of an operator compromise
criteria: No changes to any RBAC manifest or kubebuilder marker
The only pushsecrets verbs used in finalizeOpenBaoSecrets remain get and delete (both already granted by #260)
No controller-gen regeneration needed for this change
The plan explicitly calls out RBAC unchanged in review_criteria and in the PR description

requirements.description: The operator SHALL call Get on each backup PushSecret before issuing Delete in finalizeOpenBaoSecrets (CC-0091)
requirements.rationale: The current code issues Delete unconditionally (reconcile_secrets.go:185-197). Without a preceding Get + adoption check there is no way to observe whether ESO has installed its cleanup finalizer, so a racing Delete removes the object outright and ESO never runs DeletionPolicy=Delete on the KV-v2 path.

scenarios.name: PushSecret adopted by ESO
scenarios.when: finalizeOpenBaoSecrets runs and a backup PushSecret exists with the ESO finalizer pushsecret.externalsecrets.external-secrets.io/finalizer
scenarios.then: the handler proceeds to issue Delete exactly as today
and_then: Pass-2 Get-NotFound semantics remain unchanged
done=true is returned once both PushSecrets are gone

scenarios.name: PushSecret not yet adopted
scenarios.when: finalizeOpenBaoSecrets runs and a backup PushSecret exists with DeletionTimestamp zero and no ESO finalizer
scenarios.then: no Delete call is issued on any PushSecret in this pass
and_then: The handler returns (done=false, nil)
SecretsReady=False is set with Reason=WaitingForESOAdoption
The condition message names the unadopted PushSecret

scenarios.name: PushSecret already terminating
scenarios.when: a backup PushSecret exists with DeletionTimestamp non-zero (ESO finalizer may or may not be present — deletion is already in flight)
scenarios.then: the adoption check treats this as adopted and does not block
and_then: Existing post-Delete wait-for-gone behavior applies: handler returns (done=false, nil) with Reason=OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked until Get returns NotFound

scenarios.name: PushSecret already absent
scenarios.when: Get on a backup PushSecret returns NotFound
scenarios.then: the handler treats that resource as adoption-not-applicable and continues to the next name
and_then: Overall function returns (done=true, nil) when every Get returns NotFound — current idempotency behavior unchanged

requirements.description: The operator SHALL expose a distinct condition reason WaitingForESOAdoption when it blocks before Delete, separate from the existing OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked used post-Delete (CC-0091)
requirements.rationale: Conflating the two blocked states under OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked loses the diagnostic signal an SRE needs: pre-Delete blocks mean ESO's workqueue hasn't drained; post-Delete blocks mean ESO is doing the remote DeleteSecret. They have different remediations.

scenarios.name: Pre-Delete block uses new reason
scenarios.when: finalizeOpenBaoSecrets blocks because a PushSecret lacks the ESO finalizer
scenarios.then: SecretsReady is set to False with Reason=WaitingForESOAdoption
and_then: The condition message is 'Waiting for ESO to adopt PushSecret %q (cleanup finalizer not yet installed)'
ObservedGeneration equals keystone.Generation

scenarios.name: Post-Delete block keeps existing reason
scenarios.when: finalizeOpenBaoSecrets blocks because a PushSecret is Terminating after Delete
scenarios.then: SecretsReady is set to False with Reason=OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked (unchanged from CC-0079)
and_then: The existing message format and setOpenBaoFinalizerBlockedCondition helper are reused

scenarios.name: Both reasons share the same Type
scenarios.when: either blocked state is reported
scenarios.then: the Condition Type is SecretsReady and Status is False
and_then: No new Condition Type is introduced — downstream consumers reading SecretsReady continue to work

requirements.description: The adoption check SHALL cover both fernet-keys-backup and credential-keys-backup via a single iteration over openBaoBackupPushSecretNames (CC-0091)
requirements.rationale: openBaoBackupPushSecretNames is the single source of truth for the list of backup PushSecrets (see reconcile_secrets.go:147-152 and the CC-0079 design note 'adding a third backup is a one-line change'). The fix must preserve that property.

scenarios.name: Only Fernet adopted
scenarios.when: fernet-keys-backup has the ESO finalizer but credential-keys-backup does not
scenarios.then: no Delete is issued on either resource in this pass
and_then: The blocked-condition message names credential-keys-backup
After the test harness adds the ESO finalizer to credential-keys-backup, the next pass deletes both

scenarios.name: Only credential adopted
scenarios.when: credential-keys-backup has the ESO finalizer but fernet-keys-backup does not
scenarios.then: no Delete is issued on either resource in this pass
and_then: The blocked-condition message names fernet-keys-backup

scenarios.name: Both adopted
scenarios.when: both PushSecrets carry the ESO finalizer
scenarios.then: Pass-1 issues Delete on both in parallel (existing behavior)
and_then: Pass-2 wait-for-gone semantics run as today

requirements.description: The chainsaw E2E test SHALL retry the OpenBao kv-v2 path check in a bounded loop in step-6 (CC-0091)
requirements.rationale: Even with the operator fix, ESO's DeleteSecret against the remote is asynchronous relative to the PushSecret object being garbage-collected. A 1-3 s gap between step-4 (object gone) and step-6 (remote path cleared) is normal and should not re-flake the test.

scenarios.name: Path cleared within timeout
scenarios.when: step-6 runs and the remote path becomes not-found within 30 s
scenarios.then: the script exits 0 on first successful poll
and_then: Each iteration waits 3 s before retrying
The success message names each path as OK

scenarios.name: Path still present at timeout
scenarios.when: step-6 runs and the remote path is still present after 30 s
scenarios.then: the script exits non-zero with ERROR naming the remaining path(s)
and_then: Chainsaw reports the step as failed with the script's stderr

scenarios.name: Step-4 hard assertion preserved
scenarios.when: the PushSecret objects are absent from the API server
scenarios.then: step-4's existing error-resource assertions pass on the first check with no retry loop added
and_then: The retry loop is scoped to step-6 only

requirements.description: The docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md SHALL document the two-stage finalizer lifecycle and the two distinct blocked-reason constants (CC-0091)
requirements.rationale: The existing doc (lines 700-874) describes the single-stage Delete-then-wait flow. A future operator debugging 'SecretsReady=False/WaitingForESOAdoption' needs the doc to match reality.

scenarios.name: Lifecycle documented
scenarios.when: a reader searches the reconciler doc for 'openbao-finalizer'
scenarios.then: they find a description of (1) wait-for-adoption, (2) Delete, (3) wait-for-gone
and_then: The doc names both reasons: WaitingForESOAdoption and OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked
The doc explains which stage each reason corresponds to

scenarios.name: Symmetric coverage documented
scenarios.when: the reader looks for how Fernet vs credential cleanup differs
scenarios.then: the doc states both are covered by the same handler via openBaoBackupPushSecretNames with no per-resource branching
and_then: The doc links back to the relevant section of reconcile_secrets.go via function name (the existing doc already references function names, not line numbers)

scenarios.name: Rationale present
scenarios.when: the reader asks 'why the adoption wait'
scenarios.then: the doc names the ESO PushSecret workqueue race as the motivating failure mode
and_then: The doc references the CI run / incident that exposed it (or at least the feature ID CC-0091) so future readers can trace back

requirements.description: The fix SHALL NOT expand the operator's RBAC surface beyond the verbs granted by CC-0079 (#260) (CC-0091)
requirements.rationale: CC-0079 already granted get/list/watch/delete on pushsecrets.external-secrets.io. The adoption check only adds Get calls, which are already covered.

scenarios.name: No new verbs
scenarios.when: the fix is reviewed
scenarios.then: the only pushsecrets verbs used in finalizeOpenBaoSecrets are get and delete
and_then: No kubebuilder //+kubebuilder:rbac markers are added or modified
No manifest under config/rbac/ is touched

scenarios.name: No controller-gen regen needed
scenarios.when: the fix is applied
scenarios.then: make manifests produces no diff

scenarios.name: Review criteria enforce it
scenarios.when: the PR is reviewed
scenarios.then: RBAC-unchanged is an explicit review checkbox

requirements.description: The ESO PushSecret finalizer string SHALL be declared as a single named constant used by both the handler and the new tests (CC-0091)
requirements.rationale: Hard-coding 'pushsecret.externalsecrets.external-secrets.io/finalizer' in multiple files is a future-refactor hazard — ESO could rename it, and a single constant keeps one edit point (same approach as keystoneOpenBaoFinalizer).

scenarios.name: Production path uses the constant
scenarios.when: the adoption check evaluates a PushSecret's finalizer list
scenarios.then: it compares against the single exported-for-test-package constant, not a string literal
and_then: The constant is defined in reconcile_secrets.go alongside keystoneOpenBaoFinalizer

scenarios.name: Tests use the same constant
scenarios.when: reconcile_secrets_test.go adds the ESO finalizer to a PushSecret to simulate adoption
scenarios.then: it references the same constant defined in reconcile_secrets.go
and_then: A grep for the literal string outside reconcile_secrets.go finds zero matches

tasks.title: Add esoPushSecretFinalizer constant in reconcile_secrets.go alongside keystoneOpenBaoFinalizer (REQ-007, CC-0091). Value: 'pushsecret.externalsecrets.external-secrets.io/finalizer'. Single source of truth for the handler and tests. No other file edits.

Add hasESOFinalizer(ps *esov1alpha1.PushSecret) bool helper in reconcile_secrets.go (REQ-001, REQ-007, CC-0091). Iterates ps.ObjectMeta.Finalizers comparing against esoPushSecretFinalizer. Unit-test it with empty finalizers, wrong finalizer, correct finalizer, correct + other finalizers. Keep it internal to the package.

Add setOpenBaoWaitingForESOAdoptionCondition helper in reconcile_secrets.go (REQ-002, CC-0091), symmetric to setOpenBaoFinalizerBlockedCondition. Sets SecretsReady=False/Reason=WaitingForESOAdoption with message 'Waiting for ESO to adopt PushSecret %q (cleanup finalizer not yet installed)'. ObservedGeneration=keystone.Generation.

Modify finalizeOpenBaoSecrets in reconcile_secrets.go to add Pass-0 adoption check BEFORE the existing Pass-1 Delete loop (REQ-001, REQ-003, CC-0091). For each name in openBaoBackupPushSecretNames: Get → if NotFound continue; if DeletionTimestamp non-zero continue (counts as adopted); if hasESOFinalizer false → call setOpenBaoWaitingForESOAdoptionCondition and return (false, nil) WITHOUT entering Pass-1. Only if every present PushSecret is adopted does the function fall through to Pass-1 + Pass-2 (unchanged code). Update the function doc-comment to describe the now-three passes and name REQ-001/REQ-003.

Add TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets_BlocksOnMissingESOAdoption in reconcile_secrets_test.go (REQ-001, CC-0091). Seed both backup PushSecrets with NO finalizers. Use fake.NewClientBuilder().WithInterceptorFuncs() to count Delete calls. Assert: (done=false, err=nil), Delete-count=0, SecretsReady condition has Reason=WaitingForESOAdoption and message contains the first unadopted PushSecret name.

Add TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets_ProceedsOnceAdopted in reconcile_secrets_test.go (REQ-001, CC-0091). Seed both backup PushSecrets with [esoPushSecretFinalizer]. Assert: first call returns (false, nil) with both PushSecrets now having DeletionTimestamp (blocked on Pass-2 gone-wait under OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked). Clear the finalizer, call again — assert (true, nil) and both NotFound.

Add TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets_MixedAdoptionState in reconcile_secrets_test.go (REQ-003, CC-0091). Seed fernet with ESO finalizer, credential without. Assert: (done=false, nil), Delete-count=0 on BOTH resources (via interceptor), condition message contains credential name (not fernet). Then add ESO finalizer to credential, call again, assert Pass-1 Delete fires on both.

Add TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets_TerminatingCountsAsAdopted + TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets_AbsentPushSecretIsStillIdempotent + TestESOFinalizerConstant + TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets_RBACShape in reconcile_secrets_test.go (REQ-001, REQ-006, REQ-007, CC-0091). The Terminating case seeds a PushSecret with DeletionTimestamp pre-set and the ESO finalizer — asserts handler falls through to OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked, not WaitingForESOAdoption. The absent case just re-runs the empty-client setup and asserts (true, nil) — regression pin for idempotency. The constant test asserts the string literal value. The RBAC-shape test uses interceptors to record verbs and asserts only {get, delete} are used.

Update existing TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets_* tests in reconcile_secrets_test.go that seed PushSecrets without the ESO finalizer (REQ-001, CC-0091). Specifically, TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets_DeletesBothPushSecrets (lines 782-816) seeds via backupPushSecret(name) with no finalizers — after the fix it would block on adoption instead of deleting. Extend backupPushSecret to seed with esoPushSecretFinalizer by default (mirrors production where ESO has already adopted), and add a new helper backupPushSecretUnadopted(name) for the new block-on-adoption tests. Audit all existing call sites for correctness.

Add TestIntegrationKeystone_DeleteRacingESOAdoption in integration_test.go (REQ-001, CC-0091). Mirror the existing deletion-cleanup integration test (lines 2887-2985) but DO NOT call addESOFinalizerToPushSecret before deleting the Keystone. Assert Eventually: Keystone has SecretsReady=False/Reason=WaitingForESOAdoption, both PushSecrets still exist with zero DeletionTimestamp. Then add the ESO finalizer via the existing helper, assert they transition to Terminating, clear the finalizer, assert NotFound, assert Keystone gone from etcd. Use the existing setupEnvTestWithController helper and MariaDB-ready simulation.

Verify TestIntegrationKeystone_DeleteAfterESOAdoption (existing) still passes unchanged (REQ-001, CC-0091). This is a no-code task — run the existing integration test with the fix applied, confirm green, no modification needed. If it regresses, fix the handler, not the test. Record outcome in PR description.

Modify step-6 of tests/e2e/keystone/deletion-cleanup/chainsaw-test.yaml to wrap the existing `bao kv get` iteration in a bounded retry loop (REQ-004, CC-0091). Budget: 30 s total, 3 s interval (10 iterations max). Script skeleton: `deadline=$((SECONDS+30)); while [ $SECONDS -lt $deadline ]; do <existing path-check>; [ $all_clear -eq 1 ] && exit 0; sleep 3; done; exit 1`. Preserve existing ERROR messages naming the still-present path. Do NOT change step-4 (hard assertion on PushSecret absence).

Extend docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md openbao-finalizer section (existing lines 700-874) to document the adoption wait (REQ-005, CC-0091). Add a subsection 'Two-stage lifecycle' listing: (1) Pass-0 adoption wait → Reason=WaitingForESOAdoption, (2) Pass-1 parallel Delete, (3) Pass-2 wait-for-gone → Reason=OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked. Note the motivating race (ESO workqueue backlog, CI run 24842115250). State that both Fernet and credential are covered by the same handler via openBaoBackupPushSecretNames. Do NOT duplicate the existing content; extend it.

Update docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md condition-reasons reference table (if one exists — grep first) to add WaitingForESOAdoption alongside OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked with a one-line 'when emitted' description per row (REQ-005, CC-0091). If no such table exists, add one at the top of the openbao-finalizer section as part of task 5.1.

test_specifications.story: Operator waits for ESO adoption before deleting backup PushSecrets (CC-0091)
test_specifications.expected: [unit] Given a fernet-keys-backup PushSecret with no ESO finalizer and DeletionTimestamp zero, finalizeOpenBaoSecrets returns (false, nil), sets SecretsReady=False/Reason=WaitingForESOAdoption, and NO Delete action is recorded on the fake-client interceptor.

test_specifications.story: Operator waits for ESO adoption before deleting backup PushSecrets (CC-0091)
test_specifications.expected: [unit] Given both PushSecrets carrying the ESO finalizer, finalizeOpenBaoSecrets issues Delete on each (both reach Terminating under the fake client's finalizer-hold behavior); on a second invocation after clearing the cleanup finalizer, returns (true, nil).

After adding the ESO finalizer to credential-keys-backup, the next pass issues Delete on both.

test_specifications.expected: [integration] envtest-backed: create Keystone, wait for both backup PushSecrets to be created by the operator, immediately delete the Keystone CR BEFORE adding the ESO cleanup finalizer. Assert: (a) Keystone stays Terminating with SecretsReady=False/Reason=WaitingForESOAdoption, (b) both PushSecrets still exist with DeletionTimestamp zero, (c) after the test adds the ESO finalizer to both PushSecrets the operator proceeds to Delete and they reach Terminating, (d) after clearing the ESO finalizer they reach NotFound, (e) Keystone CR disappears from etcd.

test_specifications.expected: [integration] envtest-backed: same setup as the existing deletion-cleanup integration test (which adds the ESO finalizer before deleting) — assert it still passes unchanged. This pins the no-regression property for the happy path fixed by CC-0079.

test_specifications.expected: [e2e] The step-6 script polls each kv-v2 path every 3 s for up to 30 s, exits 0 on first successful poll (all paths not-found), and exits non-zero with an ERROR line naming each still-present path on timeout. The polling loop is scoped to step-6 only — step-4 retains its immediate hard assertion on PushSecret absence.

test_specifications.expected: [e2e] When a path remains present past the 30 s budget, the script emits 'ERROR: kv-v2/<path> still present after 30s' on stderr and exits non-zero, so chainsaw still fails loudly if the operator regresses.

Fails if a future change adds list/watch/patch/update calls inside finalizeOpenBaoSecrets. Implemented via fake.NewClientBuilder().WithInterceptorFuncs() counting verb invocations.

implementation_notes:
## Architecture

This is a bug fix with a tightly-bounded surface. The fix adds a single new 'Pass-0' adoption check at the top of `finalizeOpenBaoSecrets` (operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_secrets.go) that runs BEFORE the existing Pass-1 (parallel Delete) and Pass-2 (wait-for-gone). No other control flow changes. No new types. No RBAC changes.

## Control Flow

```
finalizeOpenBaoSecrets(ctx, keystone) -> (done, err)
  Pass-0: adoption check (NEW)
    for name in openBaoBackupPushSecretNames:
      ps, err = Get(name)
      if NotFound: continue        # nothing to adopt — already gone
      if err: return false, err
      if ps.DeletionTimestamp != zero: continue   # already in flight, counts as adopted
      if !hasESOFinalizer(ps):
        setOpenBaoWaitingForESOAdoptionCondition(keystone, name)
        return false, nil         # block; requeue via controller
  Pass-1: fire Delete on all names (existing code, unchanged)
  Pass-2: wait-for-gone on all names (existing code, unchanged)
  return true, nil
```

## Data Flow

```
Keystone CR Delete -> reconcileDeleteOpenBao (keystone_controller.go)
  -> finalizeOpenBaoSecrets
    -> Pass-0 (NEW): Get each backup PushSecret, check ESO finalizer present
      | present   -> proceed
      | missing   -> SecretsReady=False/WaitingForESOAdoption, requeue
    -> Pass-1: Delete each backup PushSecret in parallel
    -> Pass-2: Get each, block on first still-present with SecretsReady=False/OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked
  -> once done, remove keystoneOpenBaoFinalizer from CR
```

## Key Design Decisions

1. **Terminating counts as adopted.** If `DeletionTimestamp` is already set we do NOT re-check the ESO finalizer — by definition we (or ESO) have already started deletion, so no adoption race is possible now. This also correctly handles the case where a prior reconcile's Pass-1 Delete fired and the CR requeued: Pass-0 of the next reconcile must not re-block.

2. **NotFound on Pass-0 is continue, not return.** The idempotency property of CC-0079 must not regress. If every PushSecret is NotFound, all three passes fall through to `return true, nil`.

3. **Block on the first unadopted PushSecret, name it in the message.** Matches the existing `setOpenBaoFinalizerBlockedCondition` pattern. No need to name all unadopted resources — the next pass after one gets adopted will name the remaining one.

4. **Separate Reason constant, not a shared one.** An SRE reading `kubectl describe keystone` needs to distinguish 'ESO hasn't picked up the PushSecret yet' from 'ESO is doing the remote DeleteSecret now'. The remediations differ: pre-Delete blocks resolve themselves on ESO workqueue drain; post-Delete blocks may indicate an OpenBao outage.

5. **No deadline on the adoption wait.** The operator requeues naturally via controller-runtime; there is no scenario where a permanently stuck adoption is better resolved by force-Delete than by staying Terminating until an operator investigates. If ESO is permanently broken, the cluster has a bigger problem than one orphaned Keystone CR.

6. **Symmetric helper, not per-resource branch.** openBaoBackupPushSecretNames stays the single source of truth (CC-0079 design goal: 'adding a third backup is a one-line change'). The new code iterates the same list.

## Pitfalls to Avoid

- **Do not add a RequeueAfter.** The existing reconcileDeleteOpenBao handles the requeue via `!result.IsZero()`. Returning `(false, nil)` plus setting the condition is enough; controller-runtime will requeue on the next watch event (status write triggers it) or its exponential backoff.
- **Do not call `r.Status().Update()` inside finalizeOpenBaoSecrets.** The parent `reconcileDeleteOpenBao` + `updateStatus` already handle status persistence. Setting the condition on the in-memory keystone struct is correct.
- **Do not change the existing backupPushSecret test helper's default without updating all existing tests.** That helper currently returns a PushSecret with no finalizers, and many existing tests rely on the 'ESO has no cleanup finalizer' shape being harmless pre-fix. After the fix, 'no cleanup finalizer' means 'block on adoption'. Task 2.6 addresses this explicitly.
- **Do not edit docs/architecture/** — it is read-only per the planner role constraints. Only docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md.

- reconcile_secrets.go:147-152 — openBaoBackupPushSecretNames, iteration source of truth
- reconcile_secrets.go:228-238 — existing setOpenBaoFinalizerBlockedCondition, pattern to mirror
- reconcile_secrets_test.go:762-969 — existing test patterns to extend
- integration_test.go:2887-2985 — existing deletion-cleanup integration test to mirror
- keystone_controller.go:145-152, 364-399 — call site (unchanged)
- docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md:700-874 — docs section to extend
- tests/e2e/keystone/deletion-cleanup/chainsaw-test.yaml:217-252 — step-6 to wrap in retry
